name: Limble CMMS Maintenance Management API
what_it_does: Limble CMMS (Computerized Maintenance Management System) REST API for maintenance management, work order tracking, and asset lifecycle management. Enables AI agents to manage work order creation and assignment for maintenance operations automation, handle preventive maintenance scheduling and trigger management for asset reliability, access asset tracking and maintenance history for equipment lifecycle management, retrieve parts inventory and purchase order management for maintenance procurement, manage technician scheduling and labor tracking for maintenance workforce management, handle vendor and contractor management for service coordination, access maintenance KPI and reporting for reliability analytics, retrieve IoT sensor integration and condition monitoring for predictive maintenance triggers, manage multi-site facility and equipment management for enterprise maintenance, and integrate Limble with ERP, EAM, and IoT platforms.
use_cases: Creating work orders from IoT alert agents
Scheduling preventive maintenance from reliability agents
Tracking parts inventory from maintenance procurement agents
Monitoring equipment uptime from asset management agents
not_for: Software or IT asset management without physical equipment
Clinical equipment in FDA-regulated environments requiring CMMS validation
Very large enterprise requiring full EAM (Enterprise Asset Management)
best_when: A manufacturer, facility, or fleet operator using Limble CMMS wants AI agents to automate work order management, preventive maintenance scheduling, IoT-triggered maintenance, parts procurement, and maintenance analytics.
avoid_when: FDA-REGULATED EQUIPMENT CMMS VALIDATION: CMMS systems used for FDA-regulated medical equipment maintenance (21 CFR Part 820) or pharmaceutical GMP equipment (21 CFR Part 211) must be validated per GAMP 5; automated Limble CMMS integration for regulated equipment requires computer system validation (CSV); unvalidated automation for regulated equipment creates FDA inspection finding risk. Safety-critical equipment lockout/tagout — automated work order creation for equipment requiring OSHA lockout/tagout (LOTO) compliance must integrate with LOTO authorization workflow; automated work orders without LOTO validation create OSHA 29 CFR 1910.147 compliance risk. Pressure vessel and safety inspection compliance — automated preventive maintenance for ASME pressure vessels, boilers, and fire protection systems must comply with jurisdictional inspection requirements; automated maintenance completion without required inspector sign-off creates safety compliance violation.

auth.notes: Limble CMMS uses API key and OAuth 2.0. REST API with JSON. South Jordan, Utah HQ. Founded 2015. Private (Savant Capital, Greycroft, Riverwood Capital, $85M Series B). 50,000+ maintenance professionals. Cloud-native CMMS. Competes with Fiix (Rockwell), UpKeep, and Hippo CMMS for cloud maintenance management.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free plan available; limited work orders and users
pricing.paid_tiers: Monthly subscription; per-user and per-asset pricing
pricing.notes: South Jordan, Utah. Founded 2015. Savant/Greycroft/Riverwood-backed $85M. 50,000+ maintenance professionals. Cloud CMMS. Competes with Fiix and UpKeep.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key; documented per integration

known_agent_gotchas: FDA GMP EQUIPMENT VALIDATION: Automated CMMS integration for FDA-regulated medical device (21 CFR Part 820) or pharmaceutical (21 CFR Part 211) equipment maintenance requires GAMP 5 computer system validation; unvalidated CMMS automation for regulated equipment creates FDA 483 finding

OSHA LOTO compliance — automated work orders for electrical and mechanical equipment requiring lockout/tagout must integrate with LOTO authorization workflow per 29 CFR 1910.147; automated work orders without LOTO integration create life safety compliance risk

Jurisdictional inspection requirements — automated PM for pressure vessels, boilers, fire suppression, and elevator equipment must comply with jurisdiction-specific inspection laws requiring third-party inspector sign-off; automated completion without required inspections creates safety code violation

IoT sensor integration data quality — automated predictive maintenance triggers based on IoT sensor data must include sensor health monitoring; false positives from failing sensors create unnecessary maintenance work orders; implement sensor anomaly detection

Parts stockout impact — automated work order creation without parts availability check creates maintenance backlog; implement parts inventory check before scheduling preventive maintenance requiring specific parts

Multi-site maintenance coordination — automated work order assignment across multiple facilities must account for technician geographic availability and travel time; automated cross-site assignment without logistics validation creates scheduling conflicts
agent_readiness.security_notes: Maintenance management. SOC2. API key/OAuth2. US/EU. Equipment and maintenance data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Maintenance management. SOC2. API key/OAuth2. US/EU. Equipment and asset data.
